interpretation: IFIT1B, a protein homologous to IFIT1, is likely non-functional and does not play a significant role in the antiviral immune response. Its lack of critical function in inhibiting viral RNA translation suggests it may not be directly involved in the mechanisms underlying Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH). The retrieved evidence indicates that IFIT1B does not have a clear connection to SONFH or its pathophysiology, as it is primarily associated with other conditions such as pancreatic pseudocysts and myopia. Therefore, the evidence supporting IFIT1B as a biomarker for SONFH is weak, relying mainly on inference rather than direct biological relevance. Consequently, its potential as a clinical biomarker for early detection of SONFH is low.
relevance_summary: IFIT1B lacks a clear biological connection to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) and is unlikely to serve as a useful biomarker.

result.title: IFIT1B (Q5T764)
result.text:
Protein: Protein IFIT1 homolog B
Function: IFIT1B is likely non-functional, lacking the critical antiviral role of IFIT1. Unlike IFIT1, which is essential in the innate immune response as part of an interferon-dependent multiprotein complex, IFIT1B does not prevent the translation of viral RNAs that lack host-specific 2'-O-methylation at their 5' cap. Consequently, it probably cannot inhibit their translation by competing with the host translation machinery

result.title: IFIT1B ↔ Pancreatic pseudocyst
result.text:
Disease: Pancreatic pseudocyst
Description: Cyst-like space not lined by epithelium and contained within the pancreas. Pancreatic pseudocysts are often associated with pancreatitis.
Association score: 0.325

result.title: IFIT1B ↔ pathological myopia
result.text:
Disease: pathological myopia
Description: Excessive axial myopia associated with complications (especially posterior staphyloma and CHOROIDAL NEOVASCULARIZATION) that can lead to BLINDNESS.
Association score: 0.111
